note: mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` demonstrates the pipeline; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. Negatives are near-misses that share CI/security vocabulary but belong to a sibling (sec-iac, sec-secrets, sec-deps, sec-gate, sec-scan) or a plain debug/config action.

queries.text: can you audit our GitHub Actions workflows for security issues?
check the CI pipeline security before we give the runner deploy creds
our third-party actions aren't SHA-pinned — review .github/workflows/ for supply chain risk
harden our workflows, I'm worried about pull_request_target leaking secrets to fork PRs
the GITHUB_TOKEN in ci.yml has write-all, can you check permission scoping across all our workflows
manager wants a SLSA / supply-chain review of the build pipeline before the release
we're migrating from Jenkins to GitLab CI — audit the pipeline for hardcoded credentials and unsafe runners
review .circleci/config.yml — are the orbs pinned and are contexts scoped properly?
before I enable this new deploy workflow, sanity-check it for cache poisoning and artifact tampering
audit the pipeline — some actions use @main and I think a couple echo secrets into the logs
our Terraform gives the EC2 role s3:* — review the IAM for over-permissioning
queries.note: IaC/IAM review → sec-iac, not CI pipeline config

queries.text: scan the whole git history for leaked API keys and tokens we may have committed
the CI build started failing after the last merge — help me figure out why the tests break
queries.note: CI-related but a broken build → debug, not a security audit

queries.text: audit package.json and package-lock for vulnerable dependencies and typosquats
review our Dockerfile — it runs as root and the base image looks stale
quick security check on my staged diff before I commit
explain how our release workflow decides which environment to deploy to
queries.note: understanding workflow logic → explain, not a security audit

queries.text: full OWASP Top 10 security audit of the whole service before we ship to prod
add a caching step to speed up our GitHub Actions build
queries.note: CI feature work, not a security review — plain edit task

queries.text: the kubernetes manifests expose the dashboard on a LoadBalancer with no auth — review that
review our GitHub Actions — I think a workflow prints a secret into the build log and the token is write-all
queries.note: mentions secrets but scoped to CI pipeline handling → must beat sec-secrets

queries.text: the deploy workflow builds a Docker image and pushes it — audit the pipeline for artifact provenance and unpinned actions
queries.note: Docker appears but the target is the CI/CD pipeline itself → must beat sec-iac

queries.text: the pipeline deploys Terraform — review the infra it provisions for exposed endpoints and weak encryption
queries.note: target is the provisioned infrastructure, not the pipeline config → sec-iac wins

queries.text: did we ever commit an AWS key in a workflow file? scan the repo and history for leaked credentials
queries.note: detecting leaked secrets across code+history is sec-secrets' job even though a workflow file is mentioned
